stories.title: Fed and BOE Stay Guarded After 100 Days of Iran War
stories.summary: For several global central banks, the question of whether the Iran war poses more of an immediate danger to inflation or to growth is likely to remain open in the coming week.

stories.title: Natural diamond firms turn to blockchain traceability amid lab-grown competition
stories.summary: Hit by competition from lab-grown diamonds and declines in pricing, the natural diamond industry is launching a large-scale traceability system to further build customer trust and establish provenance of the consumer jewels, in a move that industry observers say will boost sales. De Beers Group, the world’s largest diamond producer and distributor, recently announced that the Gemological Institute

stories.title: UK, Japan Plan to Sign £18 Billion Clean Energy Investment Deal
stories.summary: The UK and Japan are expected to agree on an £18 billion ($24.1 billion) deal that they predict will create new jobs in the offshore wind, infrastructure and financial services sectors, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office said in a statement Saturday.

stories.title: Trump says peace deal will be signed Sunday after Iran said it remains cautious on timing
stories.summary: President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social that a deal to end the war with Iran will be signed on Sunday.

stories.title: CLO ETFs Boom on Higher Rates, Private Debt Woes
stories.summary: Wall Street has an answer for retail investors seeking to profit from elevated interest rates and dodge defaults in private credit: funds that buy collateralized loan obligations.

stories.title: Why Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPO
stories.summary: Elon Musk and his executives had one overarching message for the army of people working on the SpaceX initial public offering over the past six months: Faster, move faster. As the gigantic space exploration startup barreled toward a public listing after 24 years as a private company, the urgency was apparent. The rush was driven by a canny strategy: go public before the US midterm elections, with

stories.title: Trump Says He’ll Sign Deal With Iran to Reopen Hormuz Sunday
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the conflict with Iran would be signed on Sunday, a claim contradicted by Iran as the two sides still differ over key points, including management of the waterway and payments to the Islamic Republic.

stories.title: US and Iran Move Closer to Deal Despite Hormuz Skirmishes
stories.summary: Pakistan said an interim US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz could be finalized within 24 hours, raising expectations that the sides may be nearing a broader agreement after recent skirmishes near the strategic waterway. Bloomberg News State Department Reporter Eric Martin and Jerusalem Reporter Dan Williams joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. Paki

stories.title: Trump to Meet Mideast Partners on Sidelines of G7 in France
stories.summary: President Donald Trump will meet with US partners in the Middle East at the Group of Seven leaders’ summit in France next week, underscoring the outsized role the war in Iran continues to play as European allies grapple with the global economic fallout.

stories.title: Far-right activist Tommy Robinson detained under terrorism legislation

stories.title: Trump Taps Lawyer Who Helped Defend Him as Wall Street’s Top Cop
stories.summary: President Donald Trump tapped an experienced white-collar lawyer who helped defend him in a hush money case to be US attorney for the Southern District of New York, the federal prosecutor whose office handles many high-profile Wall Street cases.

stories.title: Why the US Is Investing in Quantum Computing
stories.summary: The Trump administration is making another move into venture-style investing, taking equity stakes in nine quantum computing companies. IBM, the largest recipient of the investment, thinks that quantum systems could eventually solve problems involving drug discovery, financial markets, cybersecurity, logistics, and climate science that remain beyond the reach of classical computers. But while comp

stories.title: Can Tech Justify a Trillion-Dollar Valuation?
stories.summary: The long-awaited SpaceX IPO tested the limits of modern capital markets, and is expected to be followed by OpenAI and Anthropic. Investor Steve Rattner says the scale of the valuations and fundraising involved is unprecedented, simultaneously reflecting optimism about artificial intelligence and a belief that the technology could transform the economy. The offerings also raise broader questions ab

stories.title: Global Capitalism Bets It All on AI Future Alarming Voters
stories.summary: Days after filing confidentially to go public, Anthropic, the $965 billion artificial intelligence juggernaut that’s one of the fastest-growing startups of all time, dropped another bombshell.

stories.title: Prices Likely to Stay Higher Even If Conflict Ends, ECB’s Nagel Says
stories.summary: Prices are likely to stay elevated for longer even if the war in Iran were to end soon, Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel said in an interview with Deutschlandfunk.

stories.title: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/13/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Swarovski CEO Alexis Nasard, Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security Senior Scholar Dr. Amesh Adalja, and Pacific University Political Science Professor Jules Boykoff. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Venezuela, Argentina’s Impsa Sign Deal to Work on Power Grid
stories.summary: Venezuela signed an agreement with Argentina’s Impsa SA to complete work at the long-stalled Tocoma hydroelectric dam and help restore the country’s deteriorating power grid.

stories.title: Swarovski CEO's focus on "modern luxury" to return the 131-year-old company to profitability
stories.summary: Swarovski CEO Alexis Nasard joined Bloomberg This Weekend's Christina Ruffini for a tour and discussion about the significant brand transformation the company is undergoing as it aims to modernize its luxury image by partnering with celebrities like Ariana Grande and Venus Williams, as well as incorporating popular Disney and Marvel characters. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: US Wants to Avoid Congressional Vote on Trade Deal, Carney Says
stories.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Trump administration officials have been clear that they don’t want to trigger a vote in Congress by changing the “fundamental architecture” of the North American free trade deal.

stories.title: As World Cup Begins, Health Officials Issue Warnings Amid Measles Outbreak
stories.summary: The FIFA World Cup is expected to create a "hospitable environment" for pathogens, with millions of people gathering in packed stadiums, and there are concerns about the spread of diseases such as Ebola, dengue, and measles. stories.title: This 28-year-old paid off her law school loans selling stickers and coloring books
stories.summary: Elyse Burns started selling her art on Etsy when she turned 18. Now she's paid off her student loans and did nearly $5 million last year.

stories.title: US’s Screwworm Fix Is Still a Year Away, Risking More Spread
stories.summary: The US’s best weapon against a deadly cattle parasite threatening the beef industry is more than a year away from showing meaningful results, raising concerns over how far the outbreak could spread before then.

stories.title: Poland to End Fuel Subsidies as US-Iran Peace Talks Heat Up
stories.summary: Poland is preparing to restore regular tax rates on fuels as peace talks between the US and Iran progress, Prime Minister Donald Tusk said at a press conference on Saturday.

stories.title: US blocks foreign access to Anthropic’s newest AI models over security risks
stories.summary: Washington has ordered Anthropic – a leading entity in America’s AI sector – to block all foreign nationals from accessing its latest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns, according to the Silicon Valley-based firm. stories.title: There’s a Bug in the Gold Trade as Miners Move Like Meme Stocks
stories.summary: Brian Laks has been investing in gold stocks for over a decade. To the deep-value investor, they serve as a hedge against volatility in the rest of his portfolio, a haven during geopolitical uncertainty and deliver steady returns.

stories.title: Pakistan PM Expects US-Iran Deal to Be Finalized in 24 Hours
stories.summary: The finalization of a deal between the US and Iran is expected within the next 24 hours, Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on Saturday in a post on X.

stories.title: Top Motorcycle Exporter China Urges Industry to Curb Price Wars
stories.summary: China’s motorcycle industry trade body urged companies across the sector to curb price wars and focus on higher-quality growth, warning that imitation and a flood of similar products are squeezing profits and hurting the reputation of Chinese manufacturing.

stories.title: Former ECB Rate-Setter Muller Reports for Duty as Volunteer Cop

stories.title: Proposed US-Iran Deal Hinges on Plan of Sequenced Rewards
stories.summary: A central element of President Donald Trump’s emerging Iran deal is a step-by-step approach that would see the Strait of Hormuz reopened followed by Tehran getting economic rewards each time it meets US demands.

stories.title: SpaceX Rented Out Computing After Own Teams Had Trouble Using It

stories.title: Challenges and Opportunities of Investing in African Startups
stories.summary: Investment into African startups held up in 2025, even as funding conditions tightened. According to AVCA, more than $5 billion was invested in African startups last year, with deal activity also rising. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja spoke with venture capital investor Andrew Firman from Kaleo Ventures on opportunities and challenges of investing in the continent. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Tom Mueller on SpaceX’s Rise and Space Economy
